prompt: Write a short poem (8–16 lines) about the feeling of finishing a long project. Any form you like.
response:
The last keystroke lands like a dropped coin in deep water—  
echoless, final.  
I peel my name from the margin,  
feel the document exhale without me.  

Night, once a narrow hallway,  
snaps wider than a calendar;  
hours suddenly have first names again.  
My shoulders discover they are continents  
drifting apart from the desk.  

In the kitchen I switch on a light no sentence asked for,  
pour milk that isn’t a metaphor,  
taste something sweet and un footnoted.  

Tomorrow the blank page will reopen its white mouth,  
but tonight I am the hush after the last page is turned,  
the small, astonished star that flares  
when a long sentence ends and the room is still here.
